translate Element

The <translate> element groups translation data that moves the frame in local space via vectors.

<translate
   value = string
/>

Required Attributes

None.

Optional Attributes

value

If specified, the value attribute should contain three space-delimited floating-point values comprising the vector by which to translate the frame. In most cases, value will be "0 0 0"—no translation. If there is animation associated to the frame, this attribute should be left undefined. See the examples below.

Element Information

Parent Elements matrix
Child Elements animate, keyTimes, values

Examples

A. Simple Translation Without Animation

In this example, the frame is translate 5 units along the x-axis. Since this is a simple translation, the <translate> element has no children.

<translate value="5 0 0"/>
B. Translation With Animation

This translation involves an animation that starts at 0 seconds and lasts for 3.33333 seconds. It is broken up into multiple key times (ranging from 0 to 1.) Each key time corresponds to a single translation. For example, at key time 0.5, the frame will be translated by the vector (0, 0, -0.270761). Because there is animation with multiple translations, the value attribute of the <translate> element is undefined.

<translate>
   <animate begin="0s" dur="3.33333s"/>
   <values>
      0 0 0
      0 0 -0.270761
      0 0 0.0461485
      0 0 0.0165069
      0 0 0.308852
      0 0 0.246983
      0 0 0.02557
      0 0 0.076491
      0 0 -0.0864152
      0 0 -0.120439
      0 0 0
   </values>
   <keyTimes>
      0
      0.5
      0.64
      0.67
      0.7
      0.73
      0.77
      0.8
      0.82
      0.95
      1
   </keyTimes>
</translate>
